title: senecio scorzonella
intro: Senecio scorzonella, also known as Blackroot or Tawny Cinquefoil, is a perennial herb native to Europe, North Africa, and parts of Asia, characterized by its dark green, woolly leaves and clusters of small yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones and alkaloids. The health benefits of Senecio scorzonella include its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of indigestion, diarrhea, and menstrual cramps. Senecio scorzonella is typically prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and can also be used in tincture form to extract its medicinal compounds.
